findings.title: requirements.txt lists 13 packages with no version pins (incl. torch)
findings.quote:
pandas
scikit-learn
numpy
matplotlib
torch
fvcore
einops
thop
timm
reformer_pytorch
openpyxl
seaborn
pywt
findings.claim: The dependency specification contains only bare package names with no version constraints for any package, including the deep-learning framework torch.
findings.concern: The runtime environment cannot be deterministically rebuilt; future versions of torch / numpy / scikit-learn may change defaults (e.g. StandardScaler behaviour, RNG, eigendecomposition sign conventions) and silently shift results, undermining exact reproduction of the reported metrics.
findings.resolution: Authors: pin exact versions (e.g. torch==2.x, numpy==1.26.x, scikit-learn==1.x) used to produce the paper, ideally with a lockfile or the Python version.
findings.reason: requirements.txt confirmed at lines 1-13 with all 13 bare package names and no version pins, matching the quote exactly.

findings.title: 23 of 24 channel-correlation matrices required by OLinear-C scripts are absent
findings.quote:
assert configs.q_channel_file is not None, 'configs.q_channel_file should not be None in orthoLinear'
q_channel_file = os.path.join(configs.root_path, configs.q_channel_file)
if os.path.isfile(q_channel_file):
    self.channel_corr_mat = torch.from_numpy(np.load(q_channel_file)).to(torch.float32).to(device)
findings.claim: model/OLinear_C.py loads a precomputed channel-correlation matrix via --q_channel_file (lines 42-45), but only weather_COV_channel_ratio0.70.npy is shipped; the 23 other *_COV_channel_*.npy files named by the OLinear_C scripts (including for datasets whose raw CSV IS in the repo: cars, covid, ILI, power, SP500, etc.) are not present (see _audit_code/out/check_channel_files_present.txt: 'required by OLinear_C scripts: 24 / present in dataset/: 1 / MISSING (23)').
findings.concern: The OLinear-C variant (reported in Appendix H) cannot be run out-of-the-box for 23 of 24 datasets; on a missing file OLinear_C.py:47 does not raise (see olinear-c-missing-file-assert), so self.channel_corr_mat is left undefined.
findings.resolution: Authors: ship the precomputed *_COV_channel_*.npy matrices for all OLinear-C datasets, or document that Generate_corrmat.ipynb (cell 1) must be re-run first (and provide the raw CSVs it needs).
findings.reason: Verified: OLinear_C.py lines 42-45 match the quote exactly; scripts reference 25 unique COV_channel filenames (24 distinct datasets) but only weather_COV_channel_ratio0.70.npy exists in dataset/, confirming 24 files missing (the claim says 23 missing/1 present which is consistent).

findings.title: Table 5 OrthoTrans-as-plugin (iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear) has no code in repo
findings.quote: Table 5: Applying OrthoTrans (O.Trans) to iTransformer, PatchTST and RLinear. Average MSEs are
findings.claim: The paper reports (Table 5) that adding OrthoTrans to iTransformer, PatchTST and RLinear improves their average MSE; the repo's model/ directory contains only OLinear and its own ablation variants, with no iTransformer / PatchTST / RLinear implementations into which OrthoTrans was inserted.
findings.concern: The 'OrthoTrans as a plug-in' generality claim (a headline contribution) cannot be reproduced from this repo because the modified-baseline code that produced Table 5 is not included.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the OrthoTrans-augmented iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear code used for Table 5, or point to the exact branch/commit where the plug-in was applied.
findings.reason: Table 5 caption confirmed in paper_text.txt at line 796; model/ directory confirmed to contain only OLinear variants with no iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear files, so the plug-in code is indeed absent.

findings.title: Headline benchmark datasets (ECL/ETT/Traffic/Solar/PEMS/Exchange/METR) not in repo
findings.quote:
2. Some datasets can be obtained from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l51QsKvQPcqILT3DwfjCgx8Dsg2rpjot/view?usp=drive_link) or [Tsinghua Cloud](https://cloud.tsinghua.edu.cn/f/2ea5ca3d621e4e5ba36a/).  The others can be obtained from the links in Appendix B. 
Some datasets are already in the folder ```./dataset```. 
The python script ```Generate_corrmat.ipynb``` for generating Pearson correlation matrices and Q matrices is also provided in ```./dataset```.
findings.claim: The repo ships raw CSVs + Q matrices for only 11 of the 24 datasets; the standard long-term-forecasting benchmarks (ECL/electricity, ETT×4, Traffic, Solar-Energy, PEMS×4, Exchange, METR-LA) are not present and must be fetched from Google Drive / Tsinghua Cloud / Appendix B links, then their Q and channel matrices regenerated via Generate_corrmat.ipynb.
findings.concern: Reproducing the main long-term-forecasting tables for the most prominent benchmarks requires external downloads and a manual Q-matrix regeneration step; these are standard public TSLib datasets and the sources are documented, so this is a completeness gap rather than an invalidation (filed as a question since I cannot resolve the external URLs in this sandbox).
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the external links remain live and that Generate_corrmat.ipynb reproduces the exact Q/channel matrices used for the benchmark datasets (the notebook currently only iterates time_lag in {96,192,336,720}, not the {3..60} horizons used by the smaller datasets).
findings.reason: README.md lines 48-50 match the quote exactly; dataset/ directory confirmed to contain only 12 local datasets (cars, covid, DowJones, ILI, nasdaq, power, SP500, unemployment, weather, website, wiki, Generate_corrmat.ipynb) without the headline benchmarks; external URL verification impossible in sandbox as noted.

findings.title: OLinear_C.py uses `assert ValueError(...)` which never raises on a missing channel file
findings.claim: When the channel-correlation file is absent, the else branch runs `assert ValueError(...)`; a ValueError instance is always truthy, so the assert passes silently instead of raising, and self.channel_corr_mat is never assigned.
findings.concern: Instead of a clear 'file missing' error, the model proceeds and later fails with an obscure AttributeError on self.channel_corr_mat; combined with the 23 missing channel files, this hides the real cause from anyone running OLinear-C.
findings.reason: OLinear_C.py lines 42-47 confirmed to contain `assert ValueError(...)` exactly as quoted; a ValueError instance is always truthy so the assert is a no-op silently, leaving self.channel_corr_mat unset.

findings.title: Q-matrix notebook uses hardcoded Windows paths and only lags {96,192,336,720}
findings.claim: Generate_corrmat.ipynb (the only artefact that reproduces the OrthoTrans Q and channel matrices) hardcodes per-dataset Windows file paths (cell 2 even points at an absolute D:\ user-desktop path) and its temporal-Q cell only iterates time_lag in {96,192,336,720}, whereas the shipped small-dataset matrices use lags {3,6,9,12,24,36,48,60}; the notebook must be hand-edited per dataset to regenerate the matrices actually used.
findings.concern: The provided regeneration script does not, as committed, reproduce the full set of Q matrices used in the paper without manual edits, so anyone needing to regenerate matrices for a non-shipped dataset must reverse-engineer the lag list and fix the paths.
findings.resolution: Authors: parameterise the notebook (or provide a .py) over dataset + lag list, and replace the absolute D:\ path with a relative one, so all shipped Q/channel matrices can be regenerated as-is.
findings.reason: Notebook line 182 confirmed to contain the hardcoded D:\ Windows path exactly as quoted; line 58 confirmed the temporal-Q cell iterates only time_lag in [96,192,336,720], matching the claim.
